title: rubus macrophyllus var. hypoleucus
intro: Rubus macrophyllus var. hypoleucus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan bramble, is a variety of the Rubus macrophyllus plant species that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant is used in traditional medicine and is known for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rubus macrophyllus var. hypoleucus include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and protect against oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or infusion, and is also used in herbal preparations such as tinctures and extracts, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and optimal uses.
